role_title: Product Manager, AI Governance and Admin Experience

level_signals: Owns mission-critical platform area (AI Governance) end-to-end
Partners cross-functionally with multiple product/engineering teams and GTM
Sets vision, strategy, and roadmap independently
Comp range $180K–$250K base consistent with Senior IC at a high-growth tech company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have a history of delivering impactful product launches and improvements in B2B environments.
You have fluency and point of view around AI governance.
You collaborate well cross-functionally—especially with GTM, engineering, design, and developer relations.
You have experience with MCP servers, API development, and enterprise integrations.
You have a comfort and an ongoing curiosity with using AI tools for your product development process.
You have a proactive mindset even for ambiguous problems with a drive to deliver results with urgency throughout.

scope.surface: AI Governance and Admin Experience — agent identity, lifecycle, trust boundaries, policy/permissions, model/tool controls, auditing/visibility, and admin experience for Notion's Enterprise product
primary_metrics_inferred: Enterprise adoption and scale of AI agents on Notion
Security and compliance posture for enterprise customers
core_responsibilities: Develop and execute a roadmap that empowers large organizations to onboard and scale agents into Notion's system of record with confidence
Work across multiple product and engineering teams to drive scalable systems for user and agent management, team collaboration, and enterprise visibility
Partner closely with GTM teams to understand business requirements, inform the product roadmap, and support strategic customer deals
Engage deeply with Notion's admin and builder personas to understand their challenges, workflows, and aspirations
Define agent identity, lifecycle, and trust boundaries and deliver admin surfaces and primitives to manage all of Notion's AI products at scale
Build policy, permissions, model/tool controls, and auditing/visibility for enterprise-wide agent deployment
Own the roadmap and strategy for the admin experience product

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of highly competitive compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($180K–$250K base) and competitive for the market
Mission-critical role with clear ownership of a strategic product area
Encourages applicants whose experience doesn't perfectly match every bullet
Equal opportunity and accommodation commitments explicitly stated
AI governance is a rapidly growing and high-visibility domain
red_flags: JD footer includes '#LI-Onsite' tag and mentions San Francisco / New York City pay ranges, suggesting on-site or hybrid only — fully remote candidates may be excluded
No specific years-of-experience requirements stated, making seniority expectations opaque
High buzzword density ('Agent Operating System', 'System of Record', 'agentic world') with limited specifics on success metrics
Visa sponsorship not addressed anywhere in the JD

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, scope of ownership, and cross-functional leadership expectations)
scope.business_model (inferred from 'B2B environments' and 'Enterprise Product Team')
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false from '#LI-Onsite' tag and city-specific pay range framing)
ambiguities: The JD footer includes '#LI-Onsite' suggesting an on-site requirement, but no explicit office attendance policy is stated in the body. Treated as onsite based on the tag.
Compensation range is stated as applying to 'roles based in San Francisco or New York City' — it is unclear whether other locations are eligible or what the range would be elsewhere.
No years-of-experience floor is stated for any requirement; seniority is inferred from role complexity and compensation.
